data.title: Arcadium Lithium falls on report about US concerns about Rio Tinto deal
data.description:
Arcadium Lithium (NYSE:ALTM) earlier dropped as much as 5%, though has since pared the loss to 1.5%, after a report that Congress is concerned about the lithium firm's sale to Rio Tinto (RIO) and potential China implications.

A U.S. House committee that's focused on competition between the U.S. and China is concerned that the $6.7 billion acquisition of Arcadium Lithium (ALTM) could impact the supply of lithium, according to traders, who cited a Capitol Forum report that was circulating on Monday, that quoted a spokesperson for the committee.

The Arcadium (ALTM) deal has raised some potential national security worries as Aluminum Corp of China (OTCPK:ALMMF) holds a 14.99% stake in Rio Tinto (RIO). Lithium is a key ingredient in rechargeable batteries, including those for electric vehicles.

More on Arcadium Lithium plc

  • Bidding Goodbye To Arcadium Lithium On A Sore Note
  • Lithium News: Arcadium Lithium, Q2 Metals
  • Rio Tinto Is Making A Big Gamble On Arcadium Lithium
  • Arcadium Lithium plc Q3 2024 Earnings Preview
  • Arcadium Lithium downgraded at HSBC after Rio Tinto's $6.7B bid

data.title: Gelsinger forced out at Intel after board lost confidence: report
data.description:
• Additional details have to come to light regarding this morning's announcement that Pat Gelsinger had decided to retire as Intel (NASDAQ:INTC)'s Chief Executive Officer, as Bloomberg reported the 63-year-old was given two options: retire or be removed.
  • Intel's board of directors lost confidence in Gelsinger's plan to turn the company around, the news outlet reported. The conflict was heightened last week after Gelsinger met with the board to discuss how to win back market share and narrow the gap between itself and Nvidia (NVDA), the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. He was then given the option to retire or be removed.
  • Intel did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Intel shares rose 5.2% in midday trading following the news of Gelsinger's departure from the semiconductor giant.

data.title: Markel downgraded at TD Cowen as premiums suffer
data.description:
TD Cowen downgraded Markel Group (NYSE:MKL) to Hold from Buy due to considerations including lower premiums in insurance and reinsurance, poor reinsurance performance, and reduced earnings in ventures.

"MKL's lower premiums in Insurance, mainly in select casualty lines gives us pause, as other industry leaders lean into some of the same lines," analyst Andrew Kligerman wrote in a note to clients.

In Q3, insurance net written premiums dropped 2.2% Y/Y on higher cessions and lower writings in a number of casualty lines. And with an underlying combined ratio of 101.1%, "more reductions could be in store," he noted.

Markel (NYSE:MKL) also had lower-than-expected profit from Nephila, he added.

"While MKL has significant redeployable capital and may increase share repurchases, we prefer carriers such as AXS (Axis Capital Holdings) and ACGL (Arch Capital Group) on better operating outlooks," Kligerman said.

Markel (NYSE:MKL) stock slipped 1.2% in late morning trading on Monday, while Axis (AXS) rose 0.6% and Arch Capital (ACGL) dropped 1.1%.

TD Cowen's Hold rating aligns with the SA Quant rating and diverges from the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: North Carolina regulator OKs Duke Energy request to cut customer rates by 4.5%
data.description:
Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK) -1.4% in Monday's trading after the North Carolina Utilities Commission approved a request to lower residential customers' costs by 4.5%, thanks to declining fuel prices.

Duke (DUK) said a typical residential customer in North Carolina using 1,000 kWh/month will see an overall decrease of $7.34, falling from $161.97 to $154.63, which it said is 11% below the national average of $174.21 - a difference of $235/year.

The utility said commercial customers will see an average decrease of 6.3%, while rates for industrial customers will come in roughly flat.

A similar decrease for Duke Energy Carolinas (DUK) customers will take effect January 1, when rates for residential customers drop 3.6%.

data.title: VeriSign avoids new domain pricing limits as it renews NTIA agreement
data.description:
VeriSign (NASDAQ:VRSN) avoided new .com domain pricing limitations when it renewed its agreement with the National Telecommunications and Information Administration over the weekend, which should be a positive development for the company.

"NTIA stated that it didn't have authority to set .com domain prices without both parties agreeing to any change, and even though it believed a reduction in .com prices would be in the public interest, conversations with VeriSign didn't lead to an agreement on how wholesale .com pricing should change," said Citi Research analyst Ygal Arounian, in a Monday investor note.

VeriSign inched up 1% during Monday morning trading. The Reston, Va.-based company provides domain name registry services and internet infrastructure around the globe.

"The provider of .com registry services handles more than 300 billion queries per day on average, in the face of numerous cyberthreats," the NTIA stated. "Over the years, Verisign has demonstrated itself to be a responsible steward, consistently maintaining the reliability of .com."

"At the same time, Verisign’s role as the .com registry does offer it significant power over wholesale pricing," the NTIA added. "The Agreement currently caps those prices at approximately $10 per domain per year, and would allow a 7% increase four out of the next six years ... Over the past several months, NTIA and Verisign have engaged in serious conversations, but, despite our best efforts, we have been unable to agree how wholesale .com pricing should change."

Citi said the agreement clears a significant overhang on VeriSign shares, and the volume side has stabilized but remains pressured. The bank has a $210 price target on the stock.

data.title: Bowlero rebrands as Lucky Strike Entertainment
data.description:
• As part of its expansion into water parks and family entertainment centers, Bowlero (NYSE:BOWL) is rebranding itself as Lucky Strike Entertainment effective December 12 and will begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the new "LUCK" ticker.
  • "As Bowlero Corp. continues to expand into new entertainment verticals, we recognize the need for a brand that reflects the full scope of our offerings. This positions the company to embrace a more diverse range of experiences and firmly establishes our future, furthering our leadership in the entertainment landscape,” CEO Thomas Shannon said in a statement.
  • As part of the rebranding, 75 Bowlero centers will change to Lucky Strike locations within the new two years, along with the debut of a flagship location in Beverly Hills, California.
  • Shares are 2.4% higher on Monday.

data.title: This is how the computer and electronics retail stocks are performing today
data.description:
As cyber-Monday has arrived, here is a look at five computer and electronic retail stocks:

ATRenew Inc. (RERE) – Quant rating: 4.89

Up 3.49% from the past five days, and so far, 69.79% year-to-date.

The stock is “positioned for growth” according to a recent analysis by Ivy Global Insights, after strong third quarter earnings results.

“The company’s robust cash position and innovative AI-powered technologies position it well for future growth and sustainable margins in the circular economy market,” the Ivy Global Insights said.

The company also appears undervalued and trading at a great discount.

GameStop Corp. (GME) – Quant rating: 3.17

GameStop (GME) is down 6.33% in the past five days, but up about 65% year-to-date.

The company’s topline remains weak and its accelerating revenue is declining, said Seeking Alpha analyst Florian Muller in a recent analysis.

“Despite optimistic valuation adjustments, GameStop's per share value still lies significantly below market price, making it a speculative play rather than a sound investment,” he wrote.

Best Buy Co. (BBY) – Quant rating: 3.16

Up 3.66% from the past five days, but so far, only 15.56% year-to-date.

The company just had disappointing third quarter earnings results, missing revenue and EPS targets, with sales growth down 2.9%.

“The challenging macroeconomic environment, election distractions, and uncompetitive business model against Walmart and Amazon are significant headwinds for Best Buy,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Harrison Schwartz in a recent analysis.

He also believes the company will not recover by 2027. “I forecast a long-term decline that may accelerate with economic weakness next year.”

Currys Plc (OTC:DSITF) – Quant rating: 3.00

Currys (OTC:DSITF) is down 3.92% today, although up 46.93% year-to-date.

The company has been in a slow decline since April 2021, after successfully recovering from the pandemic lows.

“Retailers, such as Currys (OTC:DSITF) have produced stunning share price growth over recent months – with the firm’s market valuation rising by 87% in the past year alone,” according to a Questor article by The Telegraph. “Its shares have been particularly popular amongst investors because the company sells products that typically experience large fluctuations in demand, depending on the consumer outlook.”

Smart Share Global Limited (EM) – Quant rating: 2.63

Up 1.72% today, and up 47.7% year-to-date.

The company has increased its revenue by 50% year-over-year, and its operating margin has reached 1.3%.

Seeking Alpha analyst Tony Investing is bullish on the stock.

“I think the company's revenue will continue to show strong growth rates due to the recovery in mobility and the relatively low base of comparison last year,” he wrote recently.

data.title: Yield curve inversion talks return with the US2Y and US10Y both at 4.20%
data.description:
The shorter end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) and the longer end U.S. 10 Year (US10Y) look to potentially re-invert on Monday as the two instruments both trade at 4.20%.

The 2s10s yield curve initially inverted back on July 5, 2022 and remained inverted for 793 days until it course corrected back on August 5.

Since the curve flipped positive, the even 0.00% level has acted as a technical level of support that is now being re-tested. The 2s10’s curve also hovers right on its 100-day moving average as well.

Furthermore, see what some Seeking Alpha analysts are saying about the status of the yield space:

SA Analyst James Picerno said: “The recent rise in the 10-year yield suggests that bond market is pricing in higher inflation risk.”

SA Analyst Crystal Allen said: “The Fed's recent rate cuts have not lowered 10-year and 2-year Treasury rates, which have recently crossed paths, indicating a potential ‘bear steepening’ and possible recession.”

SA Analyst Infrastructure Capital Advisors said: “Our models show that the Trump administration’s pro-growth policies of lowering taxes on investment and constraining consumption are anti-inflationary. Therefore, inflation fears and the recent rise in bond yields are way overdone.”

Moreover, for traders and investors looking to further track the status of Treasury yields and the fixed income space here are several notable Treasury and fixed income-focused ETFs to keep in mind:

Treasury ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), and (BIL).

Bond ETFs: (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), and (TIP).

data.title: U.K. seeks to strengthen military supply chain amid 2.5% spending goal
data.description:
The United Kingdom will stress-test the resiliency of its domestic supply chain and military as the government prepares a plan to increase defense spending, Bloomberg News reported Monday.

Defense Secretary John Healey announced plans for a Defense Industrial Strategy that would bring jobs to every nation and region of the UK. The strategy is part of a broader plan to increase yearly defense spending to 2.5% of gross domestic product.

The stress test will study how to keep forces on the front line through a range of scenarios, such as a protracted conflict and various supply chain disruptions.

“Our forces need to be better equipped and behind them needs to be an industry that is better able to produce what they need and innovate at the pace of wartime pressures,” Healey said to Bloomberg TV in London. “I’ve made decisions that are long overdue.”

The government plans to propose the Defense Industrial Strategy in the first half of 2025, with a remit to last until 2035. Investors, industry and unions will have until the end of February to comment on the proposals.
